title: AttendancePolicy.late_arrival_penalty_type — canonical patterns
description: The four penalty patterns AttendancePolicy can enforce for late arrivals. Selected per-policy; applies to every LateArrival created under that policy.

values.description: Late arrival is logged (LateArrival row created for coaching feedback) but no consequence — no pass consumption change, no notification, no fee.
values.typical_use_case: Recreational leagues where lateness is common and unproblematic.

values.description: Pass is consumed at full value regardless of lateness. LateArrival row records the pattern for coach feedback. Default for most tenants — being late doesn't grant a discount.
values.typical_use_case: Standard academy policy — a session is a session, regardless of when the athlete showed up.

values.description: Pass is marked half-consumed via metadata.fractional=0.5. A full pass effectively becomes two half-consumptions this period. Requires additional accounting on the finance side — the linked Pass's metadata tracks the fractional balance.
values.typical_use_case: Enterprise tenants offering a fairness policy — chronic lateness reduces perceived value, encouraging retention via visible flexibility.

values.description: No pass consumption. LateArrivalNotification fires to guardian + coach. Pass stays available for a future session in the period. Zero fee.
values.typical_use_case: Youth academies encouraging punctuality via feedback rather than penalty — guardians learn about the pattern; coach adjusts.
